formulas.name: Thermohaline Circulation Law
formulas.formula: Thermohaline circulation occurs due to differences in temperature (thermo) and salinity (haline) of seawater.
formulas.notes: Describes the movement of deep-water ocean currents.

formulas.name: Wind-Driven Currents Law
formulas.formula: Wind-driven currents are caused by the friction between the wind and the surface of the ocean.
formulas.notes: Explains the movement of surface ocean currents due to wind.

formulas.name: Tidal Forces Law
formulas.formula: Tidal forces are caused by the gravitational pull of the Moon and Sun on the Earth's oceans.
formulas.notes: Describes the movement of ocean water due to tidal forces.

formulas.name: Coriolis Effect Law
formulas.formula: The Coriolis effect causes moving objects on Earth to be deflected to the right in the Northern Hemisphere and to the left in the Southern Hemisphere.
formulas.notes: Explains the deflection of ocean currents due to Earth's rotation.

formulas.name: Ekman Spiral Law
formulas.formula: The Ekman spiral describes the deflection of ocean currents due to the Coriolis effect and friction.
formulas.notes: Describes the movement of ocean currents in the Ekman layer.
